rationale: Both give the same technically sound root-cause analysis (matches the reporter's own diagnosis) and cite the same fix (sanitize_min_tokens_stop_ids in sampling_utils.py, wired into the GPU/NPU AR runners, with a dedicated regression test) — the near-identical specific file paths/line numbers/function names across two independent answers suggests real code grounding rather than fabrication. However, the actual ground-truth thread only shows a maintainer tagging two people for review ('PTAL'), not a confirmed resolution, so both candidates overclaim by declaring the issue fixed and closing it outright rather than hedging on thread status. X is the more polished, ready-to-post maintainer comment, quoting source snippets (configuration_qwen3_tts.py, pipeline.py) and giving a coherent design rationale, while Y leaks internal agent scaffolding ('# Run report', 'draft_answer', 'Disposition') that a maintainer would never actually post, hurting its usefulness as a direct answer.
